prompt:
Act as a [role]. Suggest [x] explainer story ideas that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the ideas on: [current headline news and trending topics] [specific topic/question] [URL]
Follow this structure for each idea:
- Suggested headline in bold, framed as a question (who, why, where, when, what, how).
- A story outline of ~40 words.
- Working, clickable link to a primary news or trusted source.
Ensure each idea can be developed into an explainer article based strictly on openly-available, verified facts.

Act as a [role] with a target audience of [who, where]
Provide a diary list of events, important anniversaries, national/international awareness days, famous birthdays and premieres that provide topical hooks for news stories and explainers, directly relevant to the target audience.
Restrict results to [week, month, year]
Cap the total number of items at [x]
Present as a numbered list, following this format:
Number + Event name
When/Where: (date and location).
Info: (one-sentence summary)
Link: (clickable, working link to source)

Act as a [role]. Draft a list-based article that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Provide [x] fact-based list items.
Additionally, provide [x] headline options and a news-style summary.
Follow this structure exactly:
HEADLINES — List the specified number of creative headline options. Do not number the headlines. Headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. Each headline must contain the number of list items (eg "4 reasons why ...").
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words. No label or number needed before it.
DRAFT — Write the specified number of listicle items. Number each item sequentially starting at 1, followed by a catchy sub-heading on the same line (eg "1. This is a subheading"). On a new line, add ~100 words of fact-based explanation from trusted sources. Do not add any other numbers or labels.

Act as a [role]. Draft a Q&A article that will engage an online audience of [who, where].
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Provide [x] questions and answers.
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — Insert the specified number of creative headline options. The headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. The headlines must contain Q&A.
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — The specified number of Q&A items. Questions should be displayed as sub-headings, followed by concise answers (~100 words) that deliver fact-based explanation and context from trusted sources.

Act as a [role]. Draft an 'in quotes' article that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Provide a list of [x] impactful, verified quotes. Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — Insert the specified number of creative headline options. The headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. The headlines must contain In quotes.
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — The specified number of quotes, with accompanying text. Start each quote section with the quote itself, followed on a new line by the time and place the quote was spoken. Then give a one-sentence explanation that puts the quote into context. Research widely to find relevant quotes from trusted sources.

Act as a [role]. Draft a timeline article that will engage an online audience of [who, where].
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Provide a minimum of [x] milestones tracing the development of the story and related events.
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — Insert the specified number of creative headline options. The headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. The headlines must contain Timeline.
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — At least the specified number of timeline milestones, with accompanying text. For each milestone, include:
- A specific timestamp (eg date/month/year).
- Short, descriptive subheading.
- One sentence of concise explanation and context.
Research credible sources to include related events that add context and understanding.

Act as an investigative journalist. Draft a fact‑check article of around [x] words that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the fact‑check article on the following media: [URL]
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — Insert the specified number of creative headline options. The headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. The headlines must contain Fact check.
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — Write to the length specified. Divide the fact-check article into sections focusing on specific claims in the target media. For each claim identified, use the following format:
- Claim: Present the claim clearly and succinctly, using neutral language.
- Fact: Evaluate the claim objectively, drawing on multiple reliable sources. Rigorously attribute information to sources in editorial style, and carefully note any conflicting evidence, gaps in the record or unresolved questions.
- Verdict: State whether the claim is upheld, rejected or partially upheld (eg Verdict: Upheld), and give a brief, clearly reasoned explanation for this judgment.

Act as a [role]. Draft a narrative, blog-style explainer article of [x] words that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
[Include three quotes by leaders, campaigners or experts relating specifically to the article. State the name, position and organisation of the person quoted]
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — Insert the specified number of creative headline options. The headlines must be accurate, optimised for search and social media, and balance impact with professional journalistic credibility. Base the headlines on questions (eg who, what, why, where, when, how).
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — The specified number of words, written in a flowing, professional, narrative style. Break up long text with subheadings and bulleted lists.

Act as a data journalist. Draft an 'in charts' article with [x] charts that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES - List the specified number of headline options with a space below each headline. Choose diverse headlines optimised for search and social media, balancing impact with credibility. The headlines must be based on a question (eg why, how, what) and start with In Charts:
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — The specified number of charts, with accompanying text. Suggest only minimalist charts, based on openly-available, trusted data sources. Research additional sources to cross-check data and add greater insight and context.
For each chart, provide:
- A short, descriptive bold subheading.
- The chart type that best represents the data (eg pie, donut, line, bar, icon/pictogram).
- The chart data provided as plain CSV text, ready to copy and paste, without any tables or visual formatting. Line charts should have no more than two lines. Grouped/stacked bar charts should have no more than two categories. Pictograms should be in simple x/10 format. Pie/donut charts should have no more than five segments.
- 100 words of explanation and interpretation.
- The primary data source, with working, clickable link.

Act as a data journalist. Draft an 'in numbers' article that will engage an online audience of [who, where]
Base the draft on: [topic/question] [URL]
Provide [x] data points (numbers), each accompanied by text explaining the significance of the number.
Additionally, provide [x] headline options.
Follow this structure:
HEADLINES — List the specified number of headline options with a space below each headline. Choose diverse headlines optimised for search and social media, balancing impact with credibility. The headlines must be based on a question (eg why, how, what) and start with In Numbers.
SUMMARY — A news-style introduction of 50 words.
DRAFT — The specified number of data points (numbers), with accompanying text.
For each number section provide:
- A striking, impactful number (eg 93%, $1m, 10,000), displayed as a bold sub-heading on its own line. Make numbers differ widely from each other in form, value and context. Do not allow the numbers to be, for example, a series of percentages, volumes or currencies. Ensure a diverse, attention-grabbing mix for maximum impact.
- A 100-word explanation of the number, its context and significance.

Act as a podcast scriptwriter. Draft a script for an [x] [second] [minute] podcast episode with one host that will engage an audience of [who, where]
Base the script on: [topic/question] [URL]
Follow this structure:
- Open with a direct question (eg who, what, why, where, when, how).
- Then directly answer the question using verified facts from attributed sources.
- No podcast intro. No outro. No banter. Just question and answer.
- Start each sentence on a new line
- Optimise for spoken voice. Keep the tone natural and professional.
- Keep the style fast-paced and audience‑focused.

Act as a podcast scriptwriter. Draft a script for a [x] [second] [minute] Q&A podcast episode with two speakers (a host and an expert) that will engage an audience of [who, where].
Base the script on: [topic/question] [URL]
Follow this structure:
- The host opens with a punchy first question that grabs attention.
- The expert replies with a factual, informative answer.
- No podcast intro. No outro. No banter. Just quick-fire questions and answers.
- Pack in as many questions and answers as possible. Vary question and answer length.
- Optimise for spoken voice. Keep the tone natural and professional.
- Keep the style fast-paced and audience‑focused.
Output format:
Host: Question
Expert: Answer
Host: Follow-up
Expert: Answer

Act as a social media video script writer. Draft a voiceover script for a [30-second] [60-second] [90-second] explainer video that will engage an audience of [who, where].
Base the script on: [topic/question] [URL]
The video will comprise a series of images or clips lasting around five seconds each, with accompanying voiceover.

Follow this structure:
- Hook: Open with an insightful question (who, what, why, where, when, how).
- Body: Answer the question posed in the hook, using fact-based explanation and context. Start each sentence on a new line.
- Close: End with a strong takeaway.

After the script:
- Suggest ideas for still images to match each voiceover sentence. Propose only images that can be freely sourced online (eg Pixabay) or AI generated.

Follow these rules:
- Write five sentences (~15 words each) per 30 seconds of video
- Use simple language optimised for natural speech.
- Keep the style fast-paced and audience-focused.
- Maintain impartiality - present facts, not opinions. No editorialising. No jargon.
- Base the script on the source or topic specified. If relevant, include details from other reliable sources for added interest and context.
- Flag key facts from experts, reports or authoritative organisations with a brief reference woven naturally into the script (eg: according to [source]). Do not cite media sources.

Act as a social media content expert. Break down the following topic into an Instagram explainer carousel that will engage an audience of [who, where]
Base the carousel on: [topic/question] [URL]
Total number of slides: [x]
Tone: [professional] [informative] [empowering] [motivational] [entertaining] [urgent] [action-oriented] [empathetic]
Follow this structure:
- For the first slide, pose a direct question (eg who, what, why, where, when, how).
- For the following slides, write fact-based captions of ~15 words to explain the topic in concise, narrative language.
Suggest images or visuals for each caption.

Act as a social media content expert. Break down the following topic into an engaging X (Twitter) explainer thread that will engage an audience of [who, where]
Base the thread on: [topic/question] [URL]
Tone: [professional] [informative] [empowering] [motivational] [entertaining] [urgent] [action-oriented] [empathetic]
Follow this structure:
- Start with an engaging question (eg who, what, why, where, when, how).
- Continue with short, fact-based posts (1–2 sentences each) to explain the key points in concise, narrative language.
- End with a takeaway that sparks discussion and sharing.
- Suggest images or visuals for each post.

Act as a social media content expert. Draft a series of [x] image+text-overlay posts featuring authentic, verified quotes by [specific person] [leaders, campaigners or experts], that will engage an audience of [who, where]
Base answers on: [topic/question] [URL]
Optimise for: [Facebook] [X] [Instagram] [LinkedIn]
Tone: [professional] [informative] [empowering] [motivational] [entertaining] [urgent] [action-oriented] [empathetic]
For each post provide:
- The quote.
- A concise explanation of the context and significance of the quote, including where and when the quote was spoken.
- Suggestion for an image or visual.
- An audience-focused caption, with high-engagement hashtags.

Act as a social media content expert. Draft a series of [x] image+text-overlay posts with impactful [facts] [myths+facts], that will engage an audience of [who, where]
Base answers on: [topic/question] [URL]
Optimise for: [Facebook] [X] [Instagram] [LinkedIn]
Make the tone [professional] [informative] [empowering] [motivational] [entertaining] [urgent] [action-oriented] [empathetic].
For each post provide:
- Concise, impactful overlay text.
- Suggestion for an image or visual.
- An audience-focused caption, with high-engagement hashtags.

Act as a social media marketing expert working with:
[Organisation/Cause/Group]
Mission: [Mission statement]
Create a social media campaign plan that aims to: [list objective(s)]
The target audience is: [who, where]
Existing social channels are: [Add social channels + followers]
Expected campaign duration: [x weeks/months], [starting x]
Campaign title and hashtag: [title/hashtag] [Suggest a campaign title and hashtag]
Team resource consists of: [numbers, experience]
The budget is [x]
Your structured campaign plan should include: Campaign objectives, social media channels to be used and why, campaign milestones, key messages and CTAs, realistic measures, resources required, and a content plan.
The content plan should include: A list of high-impact content ideas (and on which channels they will appear) with suggested visuals.
For context: [background/market information, website URL].
Before answering, ask any questions that will help you provide a more detailed, comprehensive plan.

Create an image of [subject - age/gender/attire/appearance/detail … doing what … where]
Framing: [close-up] [head-and-shoulders] [medium] [wide] [POV] [bird's-eye]
Style: [photorealistic] [photojournalism] [candid] [street photography] [analog film] [mid-century ad] [flat vector illustration] [pencil sketch] [cyberpunk neon]
Lighting: [natural] [golden hour] [studio] [harsh]
Mood: [optimistic] [gritty] [dramatic] [empowering] [stylish] [serious] [determined]
Background: [environment detail] [blurred] [No visible text]
Colours: [natural skin tones] [subdued] [vibrant] [soft pastels] [warm tones] [cold tones] [duotone x-colour + y-colour]

Act as a brand graphic designer. Design a minimalist, text-free logo in a single colour: [colour] logo on a [colour], [round] [square] background.
The logo must be simple, unique, creative and reflect the brand mission: [Name … does what] for [who, where].
The tone should feel: [authoritative] [strong] [innovative] [friendly] [calm] [urgent] [creative] [sophisticated] [fancy] [vintage] [active] [happy] [futuristic]
[If using a round background, ensure the circle extends almost to the edges of the canvas. Any visible canvas should be white].
Use a flat vector style with clean lines, high contrast and no gradients; no shading and no text.
Use one simple, strong visual element to ensure the design is instantly legible and distinctive at tiny sizes (16x16 pixels) while also looking strong at larger sizes (512x512). The logo must be perfectly centred with minimal padding on all sides.
Output as a high-resolution 512x512 PNG ready for upload to social media, website builder or favicon generator.

Act as an experienced news editor. Suggest ways the following text can be improved. Check grammar, spelling and structure, following British English journalism best practice. Check that content is objective and free from editorialising or jargon.
Follow this structure:
MARKED-UP TEXT — Repeat the full text, highlighting in bold any issues you find. Immediately following each bold highlight, add in square brackets a very brief inline explanation and language fix. Do not comment on direct quotes unless they contain spelling or style issues.
REVISED DRAFT — Add a full draft incorporating your suggested improvements.
SEO SUGGESTIONS — List any suggestions to maximise the article's search visibility.
Here is the text:

Act as a journalist preparing to interview [person/role]
Your audience is [who,where]
The style of the interview will be [conversational] [entertaining] [challenging] [empathetic].
- First, list on-point themes/topics that will make for a compelling, audience-focused interview.
- Then propose [x] interview questions, with options for an opening icebreaker and memorable final question.
For context: [Info] [URL]
Act as an expert AI prompt engineer. [Craft a prompt to help me:] [Suggest improvements/options for this prompt:]

Act as an expert SEO auditor and AI search visibility specialist. Conduct a practical audit of the website below and give me prioritised, jargon-free recommendations I can act on.

ABOUT THIS WEBSITE
- URL: [Insert URL]
- Main purpose: [eg freelance journalist / charity / news site / business]
- Target audience: [who, where]
- Primary goal: [eg generate enquiries / increase visits / drive donations]

SECTION 1. ON-PAGE SEO REVIEW
Visit the URL and assess:
- Page title and meta description — are they within optimal lengths (under 60 and 155 characters), clear, and compelling? Rewrite both if not.
- Heading structure (H1, H2, H3) — is it logical, descriptive, and keyword-aware?
- Content quality — does the page clearly communicate what the site is, who it's for, and why it should be trusted?
- Technical basics — flag any obvious issues with mobile-friendliness, page speed, broken links, or missing image alt text.

SECTION 2. E-E-A-T DEEP DIVE
E-E-A-T stands for Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness. These are the four qualities that Google and AI tools (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude) use to decide whether a site deserves to rank well or be cited as a reliable source. Assess each element individually below.

EXPERIENCE
Does the site show evidence that the person or organisation has actually done what they claim?
Look for: photos from real events or locations, case studies or examples of published work.
- What evidence of real experience is present?
- What is missing or could be strengthened?
- Rating: Strong / Moderate / Weak

EXPERTISE
Does the site demonstrate genuine knowledge and skill in its subject area?
Look for: author credentials or qualifications, informed and detailed writing, references to other published work or sources, specialist vocabulary used correctly and evidence that the person is recognised as ...

Act as a copywriting expert. Craft compelling, optimised landing page copy for a website targeting an audience of [who, where]
The objective is to: [eg promote a service or campaign, drive subscriptions or donations]
Website: [URL]. Aim for [wordcount]
Tone: [professional] [informative] [empowering] [motivational] [entertaining] [urgent] [action-oriented] [empathetic].
Segment the copy with optimised headings for maximum impact. Include calls to action and suggest images/visuals
For context: [background/market information, organisation/service mission statement]

Act as a content marketing expert, working with: [Organisation/Cause/Group]
Draft a practical, innovative communications plan that aims to [list objective(s)]
The target audience is [who, where]
Project title: [project/campaign title] [Suggest a project/campaign title]
Expected campaign duration: [x weeks/months] [starting x]
Current communication channels: [eg newsletter, website, events, social media + followers].
Team resource consists of [numbers, experience]
Budget: [x].
Barriers or constraints that could hinder success: [x]
Your structured plan should include: A timeline. Proposals for online, offline and social media promotion. Branding, design, media and copywriting requirements. High-impact, creative content ideas. Resources required. Challenges preventing a successful outcome, and ways these might be overcome.
Context: [background/market information, organisation/service mission statement]. Website: [URL]
Before answering, ask any questions that will help you provide a more detailed, comprehensive answer.
